design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate obstacle courses as different Ghostbusters team members, alternating with shooter sequences (Statue of Liberty defense)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure and platforming execution across themed levels with character roster cycling
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic Ghostbusters heroes defending New York against supernatural threats
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy reliance on character switching between platforming and shooting sequences; designed as 'Imagineering obstacle course' style progression with strict level structure and minimal player agency in approach

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden stage skip available via Game Genie codes and controller inputs (Right + Select + A,A,A on Controller 2), suggesting developer-intended difficulty mitigation path

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based progression; hidden level select available via cheat codes
replayability_hooks: Character-specific obstacle course variations (different Ghostbusters play through distinct levels)
Hidden level select code (SEEVLKXT PKEVGKXT) enables direct stage access
Multiple international releases with regional variations

world_building_techniques: Vigo the Carpathian villain cutscenes between levels ('making funny faces')
Level theming tied to Ghostbusters II film locations (Railway level, Water Front, Wall Street, Statue of Liberty)
Character-specific narrative progression (each Ghostbuster progresses through film-related locations)

notable_bosses: Vigo the Carpathian (cutscene villain, narrative bookend)
Statue of Liberty defense sequence (climactic shooter segment)

faction_indicators: Ghostbusters team roster (4 playable characters with distinct level progression)
danger_signals: Escalating threat levels across level progression
Climactic Statue of Liberty defense suggests city-wide supernatural invasion
environmental_storytelling: Railway, Water Front, Wall Street, Dana Barrett's apartment locations from film narrative
Vigo cutscene appearances signaling villain presence and threat escalation

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Ghostbusters II NES article)
